Your Coach Marketing Business Plan is designed to help you create your optimal coach practice. Even if you�re not ready to start a coaching practice, or if you�re just working on applying SI to your current career, I think you�ll find these classes very helpful. The good news is that you get to design your practice around your strengths, desires, and interests. In most careers, you have to mold yourself to the job. In coaching, you mold your career to who you are.

We start with the basics � understand yourself, your strengths, your experience, and your interests. What does your brain tend to do naturally? What kinds of situations draw you to want to work on them? What do you love and think is important for others?

Then we focus on your clients � whom do you want to work with? What kinds of people appeal to you? Is it a demographic category (stay at home moms, empty nesters, young people) or is it a professional category (small business owners, Fortune 500 Executives, or health practitioners). Is there a stage of life or a philosophy that appeals to you (homeschooling families, pregnant women)? YOU get to choose the clients you�re going to work with.

Third, we build a bridge between what you�d like to offer and what your ideal clients need. In other words, in order to serve your clients, you need to understand them. Fortunately, we�re living in a time when this has never been easier. So we begin by doing a little research to learn where your clients heads and hearts are at right now because once you understand their needs you�ll be able to offer them solutions that meet their needs and bring them great results. You�ll not only build a solid foundation for your practice but you�ll get a lot of clarity about what�s important for you.

Be ready to take notes during these classes. Complete your action forms. If you are interested in getting the book Strengths Finder 2.0 your local bookstore or amazon.com should have it. Make sure to buy a new copy because these include an online test.
